note: ITR-1 is a compact form. Unlike ITR-2/3, it does not have separate named 'Schedule CG' or 'Schedule CFL'. All income is captured in a few key sections.

schedules.compulsoryCondition: Always — every ITR-1 filer must complete Part A
schedules.description: Personal identification information, status, filing details, bank account information for refund/demand, and regime selection.

keyFields.notes: 10-character PAN. Must match exactly with PAN card and income tax department records.
Mandatory u/s 139AA. Must be linked to PAN. If not linked, ITR may be treated as defective.
Should match exactly with PAN database. Any mismatch causes processing issues.
Used to determine age category for tax computation (below 60, senior citizen 60-80, super senior 80+).
Used for OTP-based e-verification. Must be the mobile number linked with Aadhaar.
All notices, refund intimations, and processing updates sent to this email.
Critical field. Salaried individuals can switch between regimes at filing time. Default is new regime for FY 2025-26. Choosing old regime enables deductions u/s 80C, 80D, HRA, etc.
All bank accounts of the taxpayer must be disclosed. Refund will be credited to the account nominated. Invalid IFSC or wrong account number delays refund processing.

options: 139(1) — Original Return (filed on or before due date)
keyFields.notes: Select 139(1) for timely filing. For revised return, provide acknowledgement number of original return.

commonMistakes: Entering incorrect Aadhaar number causing OTP verification failure
Bank IFSC not updated — use current branch IFSC
Not selecting the correct tax regime — defaulting to new regime when old was intended

schedules.compulsoryCondition: Compulsory if taxpayer has salary or pension income
schedules.description: Captures salary income from all employers during FY 2025-26. Data should match Form 16 (Part A and Part B) issued by employer. System pre-fills data from employer-reported TDS in many cases.

keyFields.notes: Enter name as per Form 16. If multiple employers, add all separately.
10-character TAN. Available in Form 16. Used to match TDS credit in Form 26AS.
Government and PSU employees may have different allowances and treatment.
Include all salary components: basic, DA, HRA, special allowances, perquisites, profit in lieu of salary.
HRA exemption u/s 10(13A), LTA exemption u/s 10(5), Transport allowance exemption (if applicable), etc. Only relevant under old regime.

details.hra_10_13A: HRA exemption = Min of: actual HRA received, 50% of (Basic + DA) for metro / 40% for non-metro, actual rent paid minus 10% of (Basic + DA)
details.lta_10_5: Leave Travel Allowance — exempt for 2 journeys in a block of 4 years

keyFields.notes: Gross Salary minus exemptions u/s 10. This forms the 'Income chargeable under the head Salaries' before standard deduction.
₹75,000 (new regime) or ₹50,000 (old regime). Auto-populated but verify the correct amount. Pensioners also get this deduction.
Only for government employees. Deduction = Lower of: actual EA received, 1/5th of basic salary, ₹5,000.
State-imposed professional tax deducted from salary. Max deduction ₹2,500 per year.
Net Salary minus Standard Deduction minus Entertainment Allowance minus Professional Tax. Flows to Part B.

commonMistakes: Claiming HRA exemption under new regime — HRA u/s 10(13A) is NOT available in new tax regime
Not adding salary from second employer if job was changed mid-year
Mismatch between Form 16 salary figure and figure entered in ITR — causes processing defect
Wrong employer category — affects allowance exemption calculation
Claiming professional tax deduction under new regime — not available

schedules.scheduleName: Schedule HP — Income from House Property
schedules.compulsoryCondition: Compulsory if taxpayer has house property (self-occupied or let-out). One house property only in ITR-1.
schedules.description: Captures income or loss from house property. ITR-1 allows only ONE house property. For self-occupied property, standard formula gives zero or negative income. For let-out, rental income is taxable after standard deduction of 30%.

keyFields.notes: If 2 properties, one can be treated as 'deemed let-out' (notional rent taxable). ITR-1 only allows 1 property.
Self-occupied: Nil (zero). Let-out: Higher of actual rent received or fair market rent (municipal value). Deemed let-out: expected rent based on municipal value/standard rent.
Property tax / municipal tax actually paid during FY 2025-26 (not accrued). Deductible from GAV to arrive at NAV.
NAV = GAV minus Municipal Taxes paid. For self-occupied: NAV = Nil.
30% of NAV. Automatic deduction — represents maintenance, repairs, rent collection expenses. Only for let-out properties. Not available for self-occupied.
Self-occupied: Maximum deduction ₹2,00,000 under OLD regime. NEW regime: Interest on home loan for self-occupied is NOT deductible. Let-out: Full interest deductible (no limit) under both regimes.

keyFields.fieldName: Income from House Property (can be negative)
keyFields.notes: NAV minus Standard Deduction minus Interest. Negative means loss. Note: Under both regimes, loss from house property can be SET OFF against salary income in the SAME year (up to ₹2L for self-occupied under old regime). Under new regime, self-occupied interest is not deductible so loss cannot arise from that route.

hpLossSetOff.carryForward: Remaining unabsorbed HP loss can be carried forward for 8 years and set off against HP income only
hpLossSetOff.newRegimeNote: Under new regime, self-occupied property interest not deductible, so HP loss from self-occupied arises only if municipal taxes exceed nil GAV — unusual. Let-out properties: HP loss set-off and carry-forward rules apply normally.

commonMistakes: Claiming interest deduction on self-occupied property under new tax regime — not allowed
Not disclosing rental income to avoid tax — rental income is tracked via TDS deducted by tenant (if annual rent > ₹2.4L)
Forgetting to deduct municipal taxes before computing NAV
Claiming more than ₹2L interest on self-occupied under old regime — maximum is ₹2L
Not carrying forward HP loss properly to next year if unutilized

schedules.scheduleName: Schedule OS — Income from Other Sources
schedules.compulsoryCondition: Compulsory if taxpayer has any income other than salary and house property
schedules.description: Captures all residual income not covered under salary, house property, or capital gains heads. Common items: savings bank interest, FD interest, dividend income, NSC interest, RD/TD interest, family pension.

keyFields.notes: Interest from savings accounts. Deduction u/s 80TTA (up to ₹10,000) available ONLY under OLD regime. Under new regime, full amount taxable.
Interest from bank FDs, company deposits, NBFCs. TDS deducted at 10% (if interest > ₹40,000 per bank per year for non-senior citizens). Include gross amount and claim TDS credit separately.
Post-abolition of DDT (from FY 2020-21), all dividends are taxable at slab rate. Include dividends from all companies and MFs. TDS deducted at 10% if dividend > ₹5,000 from a company.
Pension received by family member after death of government employee. Taxable as other sources. Standard deduction u/s 57(iia): Lower of ₹15,000 or 1/3 of family pension.

NSC interest accrues annually and is deemed reinvested — taxable in the year of accrual (under accrual basis) but also qualifies for 80C deduction (as reinvestment). Complex area — verify with Form 26AS.
Sum of all other source incomes.

commonMistakes: Not reporting FD interest because TDS was deducted — TDS is not final tax; must report gross interest
Omitting savings bank interest — banks report to AIS (Annual Information Statement) which is cross-checked
Not reporting dividend income from mutual funds — AMCs report to AIS
Claiming 80TTA savings bank interest deduction under new tax regime — NOT available

schedules.scheduleName: Schedule VI-A — Deductions under Chapter VI-A
schedules.compulsoryCondition: Compulsory only if deductions are claimed under old tax regime. Under new tax regime, most deductions are not available — this schedule is largely inapplicable.
schedules.description: Captures all deductions from gross total income under Chapter VI-A sections (80C, 80CCC, 80CCD, 80D, 80E, 80G, etc.). Available ONLY under old tax regime (most deductions).
schedules.regimeNote: Under NEW tax regime: Only 80CCD(2) (employer NPS) is available. All other deductions (80C, 80D, 80E, 80G, etc.) are NOT available.

keyFields.fieldName: Section 80C — Life Insurance, PF, PPF, ELSS, NSC, Tuition Fees, etc.
keyFields.notes: Maximum ₹1,50,000. Includes life insurance premium, EPF, PPF, NSC, 5-year FD, ELSS MF, tuition fees (2 children), home loan principal repayment. Old regime only.
Premium paid to LIC or any other insurer's approved pension fund. Clubbed within the ₹1.5L limit of 80C+80CCC+80CCD(1). Old regime only.
NPS contribution by employee — up to 10% of salary (basic + DA). Clubbed within the ₹1.5L overall cap of 80C+80CCC+80CCD(1). Old regime only.
Additional NPS contribution over and above the 80C limit. Separate deduction of up to ₹50,000. Old regime only.

keyFields.limit_percentage: 14% of salary (central/state govt) or 14% (private employees from FY 2025-26)
keyFields.notes: Deduction for employer's contribution to employee's NPS account. Available under BOTH old and new regimes. FY 2025-26: Limit raised to 14% for private employees (was 10%). Not subject to ₹1.5L cap.

Self/spouse/children: up to ₹25,000 (₹50,000 if any is senior citizen). Parents: up to ₹25,000 additional (₹50,000 if parents are senior citizens). Preventive health check-up: ₹5,000 within the above limits. Old regime only.

Interest on education loan for higher education of self, spouse, children, or student for whom taxpayer is legal guardian. No upper limit on deduction. Deductible for 8 years from year of first repayment. Old regime only.

Donations to approved institutions. 100% deduction (PM's National Relief Fund, etc.) or 50% deduction (subject to 10% of adjusted GTI cap). Donations above ₹2,000 must be made by cheque/NEFT/digital mode. Old regime only.
Deduction for savings account interest — max ₹10,000. Only for individuals below 60. Senior citizens use 80TTB instead. Old regime only.
Deduction for interest from bank deposits (savings + FD + RD) — max ₹50,000 — for senior citizens (60+). Cannot claim both 80TTA and 80TTB. Old regime only.

commonMistakes: Claiming 80C deductions under new tax regime — not permitted
Claiming 80D premium for cash payments — insurance premium paid in cash is NOT deductible
Exceeding the ₹1.5L cap for combined 80C+80CCC+80CCD(1)
Claiming 80G deduction for cash donations above ₹2,000
Claiming 80TTA for FD interest — 80TTA covers only SAVINGS bank interest, not FD interest

schedules.compulsoryCondition: Compulsory if TDS deducted on salary by employer(s)
schedules.description: Captures TDS deducted on salary income. Data should match Form 16 Part A and Form 26AS. Pre-filled from employer TDS returns.

keyFields.notes: 10-character TAN from Form 16
Gross salary reported by employer in TDS return
Tax deducted at source by employer. Must match Form 26AS / AIS. Any mismatch requires reconciliation.

commonMistakes: Mismatch between TDS claimed and TDS in Form 26AS — causes processing notice
Not adding TDS from second employer if job changed mid-year
Claiming TDS that was deducted but not deposited by employer — only deposited TDS appears in Form 26AS

schedules.scheduleName: Schedule TDS2 — TDS on Other Income
schedules.compulsoryCondition: Compulsory if TDS deducted on any income other than salary (FD interest, dividends, professional fees, etc.)
schedules.description: Captures TDS on non-salary income — FD interest TDS, dividend TDS, rental income TDS, etc.

keyFields.notes: Must match Form 26AS / AIS

schedules.scheduleName: Schedule IT — Details of Advance Tax and Self-Assessment Tax
schedules.compulsoryCondition: Compulsory if advance tax or self-assessment tax paid during FY 2025-26
schedules.description: Captures advance tax paid (4 installments during FY) and self-assessment tax paid before filing ITR.

keyFields.notes: From Challan 280 (physical) or online payment acknowledgement
7-digit BSR code of bank branch where tax was deposited

schedules.compulsoryCondition: Always — summary of all income heads
schedules.description: Auto-computed summary of income from all heads (salary, house property, other sources). Feeds into deduction calculation.

keyFields.notes: From Schedule S
From Schedule HP (can be negative loss)
From Schedule OS

schedules.scheduleName: Part B-TTI — Computation of Total Income and Tax Thereon
schedules.description: Final computation: Gross Total Income minus deductions = Taxable Income. Tax computed at applicable rates. TDS, advance tax, and rebate u/s 87A deducted to arrive at tax payable or refund due.

keyFields.notes: From Schedule VI-A. Zero under new regime (except 80CCD2).
Computed at applicable slab rates (new or old regime)
10% if income > ₹50L; 15% if > ₹1Cr; etc. Most ITR-1 filers (income ≤ ₹50L) pay zero surcharge.
4% of (Tax + Surcharge)
New regime: Up to ₹60,000 if taxable income ≤ ₹12L. Old regime: Up to ₹12,500 if taxable income ≤ ₹5L. Auto-computed.
Total TDS from Schedule TDS1, TDS2, and TCS credits
From Schedule IT
Positive = tax payable (pay before filing). Negative = refund. Refund credited to nominated bank account.
keyFields.formula: TTI-TAX + TTI-SURCHARGE + TTI-CESS - TTI-87A - TTI-TDS - TTI-ADVANCE-TAX
